evals.name: casual-astrology-seo-automation
evals.prompt: I have three projects: 1) a static HTML/CSS website for my herbal business that needs design polish and SEO, 2) I want to start offering SEO as a service to clients, 3) eventually I want to automate business stuff like CRM and invoicing. Right now I have too many skills activated and I see duplicate SEO tools. Can you audit and clean this up?
evals.expected_output: Audit matrix showing ~15 skills to keep (design, SEO specialist tools, content), ~28 to disable (small-business duplicates, generic marketing), identification of exact duplicates (code-review vs engineering:code-review), MCPs to authenticate (GitHub, Notion, Linear), and proposed .claude/settings.json config

evals.name: json-structured-input
evals.expected_output: Audit matrix organized by project focus (design tools for content-site, visualization for dashboard, backend for API), identification that dashboard + backend projects reduce utility of small-business and design tools, proposed config disabling tools irrelevant to these three projects, MCPs list including GitHub + Datadog focus

evals.name: minimal-input-single-project
evals.prompt: I just have one project: a marketing landing page. I don't need all these tools. What should I keep?
evals.expected_output: Audit focused on landing page needs (design, UX, SEO, content, brand voice), disables everything unrelated (backend engineering, automation, data viz), produces minimal but focused recommended skills list
